title: Auto-reload the Dashboard page on data fetch failure

requirements.summary: On the Dashboard page, automatically retry fetching stats data when the actor is not initialized or the fetch fails, without requiring manual user refresh. Replace manual reload prompts with an automatic retry mechanism using React Query options or polling, showing a loading indicator during retries.
acceptanceCriteria: When the Dashboard page mounts and the actor is not yet ready, the page automatically retries the stats fetch at a short interval without user interaction.
A loading spinner or skeleton is shown while the retry is in progress.
Once the backend actor is initialized and getStats returns data, the Dashboard renders the stats without requiring a manual page refresh.
No manual 'Reload' or 'Retry' button is required — recovery is fully automatic.
If the fetch ultimately fails after retries, a non-crashing fallback error UI is shown.

file_operations.description: Modify the useGetStats hook to enable automatic retry with React Query options. Configure retry: 10, retryDelay: (attemptIndex) => Math.min(1000 * 2 ** attemptIndex, 10000), and refetchInterval: 3000 to automatically poll and recover when the backend actor becomes available. Ensure the hook returns { data, isLoading, isError, error } so the Dashboard can render appropriate loading and error states during automatic retries.

Update the Dashboard page to consume the enhanced useGetStats hook. Remove any manual reload or retry button. While isLoading is true, display a loading spinner or skeleton UI. Once data is successfully fetched, render the analytics dashboard (total posts, category breakdown, top posts, year distribution chart). If isError remains true after all retries, show a non-crashing fallback error message without blocking the UI. Ensure the page automatically recovers and displays stats as soon as the backend actor is initialized, without requiring user interaction.
